channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Perez's homer off Rodón sparks 4-run 4th inning and Royals beat Yankees 4-2 in Game 2 to tie ALDS
item.description: Salvador Perez homered leading off the fourth inning to spark a four-run rally against Carlos Rodón, and the Kansas City Royals beat the New York Yankees 4-2 to even their AL Division Series at one game apiece.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Column: The level up goes beyond Caleb Williams
item.description: The Chicago Bears' offense has found success with Caleb Williams at quarterback, DJ Moore at wide receiver, D'Andre Swift and Roschon Johnson at running back, and a revamped offensive line, leading to a 36-10 victory over the Carolina Panthers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Carpenter's 3-run homer off Clase sends gritty Tigers to 3-0 win over Guardians in Game 2 of ALDS
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Kerry Carpenter hit a three-run homer off Cleveland's All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase in the ninth inning, and the Detroit Tigers stunned the Guardians 3-0 in Game 2 on Monday to even their AL Division Series at one game apiece.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton forces NFL, NHL and other sporting events to adjust game plans
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are planning to relocate to the New Orleans area to prepare for Sunday’s game against the Saints with Hurricane Milton expected to make landfall along Florida’s west coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel Soccer fires back at Palestinian officials after suspension proposal
item.description: The Israel Football Association (IFA) provided a statement to Fox News Digital in which it addressed allegations of discrimination by the Palestine federation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Billie Jean King named grand marshal for the 136th Rose Parade on Jan. 1
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Tennis great Billie Jean King will preside over the 136th Rose Parade as grand marshal next year.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: College Football Playoff projections: Boise State with pole position for Group of Five berth as BYU remains on the bubble
item.description: Texas remains on track for the No. 1 seed with Ohio State close behind in advance of the Buckeyes' showdown at Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Stenhouse on winning Talladega: 'We did everything right today'
item.description: Ricky Stenhouse Jr. secured a win at Talladega Superspeedway in a photo finish at the YellaWood 500.
item.dc:creator: Dee Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prescott throws TD pass to Tolbert with 20 seconds left to lift Cowboys past Steelers, 20-17
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Dak Prescott drifted to his left, then drifted some more. On a seemingly endless night, what was another couple of seconds going to hurt? With a flick of the wrist as T.J.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Big Sho: Ohtani hits tying 3-run homer in playoff debut for the Los Angeles Dodgers in NLDS win
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani rewrote the record book during his first season with the Los Angeles Dodgers. Now, he's getting a chance to make more history in his highly anticipated playoff debut.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Padres fandom in full force at first of three postseason watch parties
item.description: The Padres' quest for postseason glory continues and fans are along for the thrilling journey. Despite Saturday night's game being held in Los Angeles at Dodgers Stadium, the first of three watch parties kicked off with a packed house at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Mets' win sends ticket prices soaring for playoff matchup with Phillies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- Pete Alonso's three-run homer sent the New York Mets to a showdown with division rival Philadelphia -- and playoff ticket prices soaring.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Mets advance in playoffs with 4-2 win over Brewers
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Pete Alonso hit a go-ahead, three-run homer off All-Star closer Devin Williams in the ninth inning and the New York Mets beat the Milwaukee Brewers 4-2 on Thursday night to win their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Backlash over San Jose State University transgender volleyball player 'isn't about sports,' advocates say
item.description: (KRON) -- Four teams forfeited matches against the San Jose State University women's volleyball team since a lawsuit was filed on behalf of several NCAA athletes, including SJSU's team captain. According to the lawsuit filed on September 23, one of SJSU's volleyball players is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Lightning prospects fight for roster spots ahead of Panthers preseason matchup
item.description: Preseason means players fighting for a roster spot get a chance to impress coaches. Center Conor Geekie is one of those players who head coach Jon Cooper thinks highly of.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jazz new additions bring experience and leadership
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz did not land the "big game" Danny Ainge talked about in April, but they did sign a few key veteran role players this summer. Patty Mills is about to begin his 16th NBA season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Legendary Dodgers pitcher, broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela hospitalized
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized ahead of the team's playoff run. The popular Spanish-language broadcaster and former Dodger star pitcher stepped away from his playcalling duties last week, though no reason was given at the time, according to the Orange County Register.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5
